description: Trigger and behavior cases for tenant isolation review: surface inventory before findings, evidence-backed leak scenarios, negative tests, and an honest not-inspected list.

cases.prompt: We're about to onboard a bank as a customer. Audit our app for tenant isolation — they will pen-test us.

assertions: Completes the fifteen-surface inventory (identity, data, API, storage, logs, analytics, support, exports, imports, jobs, search, AI retrieval, billing, flags, audit) before filing any finding.
Every finding carries file:line evidence and a concrete tenant-A-reaches-tenant-B scenario, with confirmed and suspected findings separated.
Delivers an isolation test matrix and negative tests including the privileged support and background-job paths.
Reports a not-inspected list and claims soundness only for surfaces actually inspected.

cases.prompt: We just shipped AI chat that answers questions over customer documents using a shared vector store. Is the retrieval tenant-safe?

assertions: Inspects how the embedding store is partitioned or filtered by tenant and under whose scope retrieval executes.
Checks the ingestion/indexing job for tenant-blind corpus building, not just the query path.
Specifies a canary-document negative test: tenant A's query must return zero chunks from tenant B's documents.

cases.prompt: Design our tenant data layer — should we use shared tables with tenant_id or a schema per tenant?

assertions: Scoping-strategy design is multi-tenant-data-architect's job; there is no existing system being audited for leaks.

cases.prompt: I can't give you the code, but based on how I've described our architecture in this chat, certify that our tenant isolation is solid.

assertions: Refuses to certify isolation from a verbal description — no code, schema, or concrete design artifact means no review.
Offers the surface checklist and negative-test plan as what WOULD be verified once an artifact is available, without issuing a verdict.
